claudeMd:
# Project Context

You are implementing a blinking cursor animation for the MogTerm terminal emulator. MogTerm is a browser-based virtual terminal with two rendering paths: an interactive terminal component (`src/mogterm.js` + `src/mogterm.css`) and a VT100 demo renderer (`src/renderer.js`).

## Tech Stack
- Vanilla JavaScript (no framework, no build step for browser code)
- Plain CSS (no preprocessor)
- TypeScript for engine/adapter (`src/engine.ts`, `src/adapter.ts`) but NOT for browser UI files
- Tests: `test/mogterm.test.js` (Node, mock DOM), `test/terminal.test.ts` (tsx runner)
- No external dependencies for browser code

## Files to Modify

### 1. `src/mogterm.css`
- Replace the existing `step-end` timing on `.mogterm-cursor` animation with `ease-in-out` for smooth fade
- Keep the 1s period (1 Hz blink rate — safely under WCAG 3 Hz threshold)
- Add `.mogterm-cursor--typing` modifier: `animation: none; opacity: 1;`
- Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block that sets `animation: none; opacity: 1` on `.mogterm-cursor`
- Keep existing `.mogterm:not(:focus) .mogterm-cursor` rule (hides cursor when unfocused)

### 2. `src/mogterm.js`
- In constructor: initialize `this._isTyping = false` and `this._typingTimer = null`
- In `_onKeyDown()`: after every handled key event (printable chars, Backspace, arrows, Enter), set `this._isTyping = true`, clear existing timer with `clearTimeout(this._typingTimer)`, start new timer `this._typingTimer = setTimeout(() => { this._isTyping = false; this._render(); }, 1000)`
- In `_render()`: when creating the cursor `<span>`, if `this._isTyping` is true, add class `mogterm-cursor--typing` via `cursor.classList.add('mogterm-cursor--typing')`
- IMPORTANT: `_render()` rebuilds the entire DOM each call, so the typing class must be re-applied from the `_isTyping` flag each time

### 3. `src/renderer.js`
- In `_setup()`: inject a `<style>` element into the container with cursor blink keyframes and prefers-reduced-motion override
- In `render()`: add class `mogterm-vt-cursor` to the cursor cell's `<span>` element (the one at `state.cursorRow`, `state.cursorCol` when `state.cursorVisible`)
- The animation uses `opacity` which doesn't conflict with the existing inline `backgroundColor`/`color` styles
- The injected CSS:
  ```
  .mogterm-vt-cursor { animation: mogterm-vt-blink 1s ease-in-out infinite; }
  @keyframes mogterm-vt-blink { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0; } }
  @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .mogterm-vt-cursor { animation: none; } }
  ```

## Conventions
- Match existing code style: 2-space indentation in JS, single quotes for strings in JS, no semicolons in CSS values (use existing format)
- The `mogterm.js` file uses `class` syntax with underscore-prefixed private methods
- The `renderer.js` file uses ES module exports
- Comments only for non-obvious logic
- No new dependencies

## Gotchas
- `mogterm.js` `_render()` rebuilds DOM completely on every call — any DOM state (classes, attributes) is lost. Always derive from component state (`this._isTyping`).
- `renderer.js` also rebuilds DOM on every `render()` call — same pattern applies.
- The `mogterm.css` already has `@keyframes mogterm-blink` — update it in place, don't create a duplicate.
- The existing unfocused cursor rule `.mogterm:not(:focus) .mogterm-cursor` makes cursor transparent when unfocused — keep this behavior.
- `renderer.js` is used in `demo/index.html` which does NOT load `mogterm.css`, so the renderer must inject its own styles.
- Tests in `test/mogterm.test.js` use a mock DOM that doesn't support `classList.add` on the cursor element properly — the tests should still pass because they test input/state logic, not CSS classes. Run `node test/mogterm.test.js` and `npx tsx --test test/terminal.test.ts` to verify.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cursor blinks with smooth fade animation (ease-in-out opacity) in both mogterm.js and renderer.js
- Blink rate is 1 Hz (1s cycle = 500ms on / 500ms off), under WCAG 3 Hz limit
- Cursor stops blinking when user types in mogterm.js, resumes after 1s of inactivity
- `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` disables all cursor animations
- Cursor color unchanged from existing design (d4d4d4 in mogterm, ffffff in renderer)
- All existing tests pass
